from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about a sports team (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language templates (full sentence)
    templates = {
        "numberOfMembers": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} members",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"{s} played in the {o} season",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "fullName": lambda s, o: f"The full name of {s} is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"The chairman of {s} is {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"The owner of {s} is {o}",
        "nickname": lambda s, o: f"{s} is nicknamed {o}",
        "chairmanTitle": lambda s, o: f"The {o} of {s}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "part": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes {o}",
        "mayor": lambda s, o: f"The mayor of {s} is {o}",
        "champions": lambda s, o: f"The champions of {s} are {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"The leader party of {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} was in youth club {o}",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{s} is operated by {o}",
        "tenant": lambda s, o: f"{s} is tenant of {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the city of {o}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the state of {o}",
    }

    # Build clauses, merging chains where the object of one triple is the subject of others
    # First, index triples by their subject for quick lookup
    subject_index: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
    for idx, tr in enumerate(triples):
        subject_index.setdefault(tr.subject, []).append(idx)

    clauses: list[str] = []
    consumed: set[int] = set()          # triples already used as secondary information

    for i, t in enumerate(triples):
        if i in consumed:
            continue

        # primary clause for the current triple
        fmt_main = templates.get(t.predicate)
        main_clause = fmt_main(t.subject, t.object) if fmt_main else f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}"

        # find all secondary triples whose subject equals the object of the primary triple
        secondary_idxs = subject_index.get(t.object, [])
        rel_clauses: list[str] = []
        for sec_idx in secondary_idxs:
            if sec_idx == i or sec_idx in consumed:
                continue
            sec = triples[sec_idx]
            fmt_sec = templates.get(sec.predicate)
            # Build a relative clause that starts with "who"
            rel = fmt_sec("who", sec.object) if fmt_sec else f"who {sec.predicate} {sec.object}"
            rel_clauses.append(rel)
            consumed.add(sec_idx)

        if rel_clauses:
            # join multiple relative clauses with "and"
            if len(rel_clauses) == 1:
                main_clause = f"{main_clause}, {rel_clauses[0]}"
            else:
                combined_rel = " and ".join(rel_clauses)
                main_clause = f"{main_clause}, who {combined_rel}" if not rel_clauses[0].startswith("who ") else f"{main_clause}, {combined_rel}"
        clauses.append(main_clause)

    # Combine all primary clauses into one fluent sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence_body = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence_body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    # Ensure proper punctuation
    sentence = sentence_body.strip()
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
